change_summary: EXP-229 integrated: finding reframed for 2024 scope, detail_verdict_text adds FSF genuine-geometry concession plus pre-v45 evolution-log admission plus Section 1.8 n(r) cross-ref, detail_evidence gains dome-removal annotation. tldr_verdict rewritten content. detail_extra, tldr_evidence, claim, verdict, detail_claim unchanged.
our_argument_summary: Verdict Std Model Explains. Chapman (1933) Sq current physics + Kim & Chang (2018) 39-eclipse INTERMAGNET statistical confirmation predict 5-30 nT eclipse-driven geomagnetic variations; the 9-station ±10 nT 2024 observation falls inside that envelope without any aetheric input. Post-EXP-229 text concedes FSF = H(r)/r is a genuine geometric derivation (Pearson r ≈ 0.9999) but retains three architectural critiques: (a) the pre-FSF dome predecessor got 2024 latitudes wrong by the dome author's own evolution log, (b) the absolute magnitude is imported from the 2017 Boulder observation rather than derived, (c) the dome coordinates FSF feeds on are contaminated by the n(r) refractive medium per Section 1.8. The dome-removal annotation preempts the 'refuting a claim already dropped' deflection.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: 03-predictions.txt records the 2026 eclipse formula delta_Z = baseline × coverage × FSF with FSF = H(r)/r from V12 dome geometry. The 2024 WIN-025 row asserts retrospective confirmation of the 9-station ±10 nT observation from Chernogor & Holub (2024); 02-wins.txt line 27 claims that confirmation routes to WIN-010 + WIN-025. The dome author removed WIN-025 (and WIN-010, PROS-003) on 2026-03-15 per detail_extra and FAIL-007; WIN-010 was later re-confirmed on quiet-day 2017 Boulder data, WIN-025 was not re-instated.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: For WIN-025 the 'observation' is the peer-reviewed Chernogor & Holub 9-station number; no monitor.py fetch drives the WIN-025 display. The 2026 forward prediction (delta_Z ≈ −29 nT at Boulder-equivalent anchor) is in monitor.py but is not what WIN-025 claims to confirm.
data_sources_used: Chernogor & Holub 2024 Advances in Space Research
Kim & Chang 2018 Advances in Space Research (cluster-imported via EXP-229)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: The cluster-wide hedge is that coverage × FSF adjusts station amplitude without ever committing to an absolute-magnitude derivation — the anchor is always an observed value (2017 BOU or W004). That hedge survives EXP-229.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: Minor: the detail_verdict_text asserts that 'FSF = H(r)/r was introduced specifically to repair' the pre-v45 latitude-uniform failure. Per raw-text/03-predictions.txt, FSF = H(r)/r is labeled V12 dome geometry and therefore predates v31's failure. What was introduced in response to v31 was the eclipse-specific delta_Z formula that applies FSF to the 2024/2026 eclipse geometry. The 'introduced to repair' phrasing is historically loose and can be attacked.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: WIN-025 is a retrospective confirmation row; monitor.py's eclipse automation is tied to the 2026 forward prediction, not the 2024 retrospective. Keeping 'none' for WIN-025.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Chapman (1933) solar quiet (Sq) current mechanism plus Kim & Chang (2018) ensemble predict 5-30 nT eclipse-driven surface variations; Chernogor & Holub themselves attribute the 2024 observation to ionospheric conductivity change. Dome renames cause as aetheric without a different predicted magnitude.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Chernogor & Holub paper was accepted October 2024. WIN-025 row in 02-wins.txt adopts the ±10 nT figure as confirmation without recording a pre-observation numeric prediction. The dome's own evolution log records that the pre-v45 predecessor predicted eclipse magnitudes identically across latitudes — i.e., the latitude-scaled prediction was written AFTER the pattern was observed.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Binary tag aggregates over the full WIN-025 prediction. FSF = H(r)/r station-to-station scaling IS a dome-geometric derivation (conceded in detail_verdict_text). Absolute magnitude at Boulder is imported from the 2017 observation and not derived. The tag stays false because the headline quantitative claim (the ±10 nT pattern matching Chernogor & Holub) is dominated by the observation-anchored magnitude, not by the scaling ratio.

holes_found.description: FINDING FIELD STILL CARRIES 2026-ARCHITECTURE TANGENT AND OMITS THE EVOLUTION-LOG ADMISSION. Current finding: 'Eclipse-induced ±10 nT magnetic variations are predicted by standard ionospheric Sq current physics (Chapman 1933; Kim & Chang 2018). The dome derives the station-to-station FSF ratio from its own geometry but provides no absolute-magnitude prediction for 2024 — the observed 9-station pattern matches what Chapman Sq physics predicts independently. The FSF absolute magnitude is imported from the 2017 BOU observation and reused as the 2026 baseline input.' The middle sentence was reframed for 2024 (good, addresses c3 H2 partially). But the closing sentence still pivots to '2026 baseline input' — a forward-prediction architecture point — while WIN-025 is the 2024 retrospective row. c3 H1 flagged the strongest available attack: dome's own evolution log (raw-text/06-evolution.txt line 21) admits the pre-v45 predecessor 'Incorrectly predicted 2024 eclipse magnitudes identically across all latitudes.' That admission made it into detail_verdict_text but NOT into the finding row that most readers see. The finding would be stronger if the closing sentence replaced the 2026-baseline tangent with: 'The dome's own evolution log admits the pre-FSF predecessor got 2024 latitudes uniformly wrong.' The summary-table row is the single line a skim-reader carries away; it should hit WIN-025's strongest point, not a forward-year side point.
holes_found.recommendation: Edit finding closing sentence. Draft: '...matches what Chapman Sq physics predicts independently, and the dome's own evolution log admits the pre-FSF predecessor got 2024 eclipse latitudes uniformly wrong (v31 entry, raw-text/06-evolution.txt).' This keeps the finding WIN-025-scoped (2024) and imports the single strongest admission from the expanded text up to the summary row.

holes_found.description: INTERNAL Kp INCONSISTENCY BETWEEN detail_evidence AND detail_extra / FAIL-007. detail_evidence asserts the Chernogor & Holub 2024 9-station observation was 'under quiet geomagnetic conditions (Kp ≤ 3).' detail_extra and data/uncounted-failures.json FAIL-007 both cite the dome author's own timing audit recording 'geomagnetic storm contamination during the April 8, 2024 eclipse (Kp ~5-6).' Same event, same paper's data window, two irreconcilable Kp claims in our own text. A dome defender reading both sides will flag this instantly — either we have the Kp wrong in detail_evidence or we have silently suppressed the subset/time-window-selection step that lets both be simultaneously true (e.g., the paper selected a subset of stations or a narrow time slice inside a broader disturbed window). Either way this undermines the rhetorical punch of 'this is clean quiet-day data and still matches standard physics' because the dome author himself contests 'clean.'
holes_found.recommendation: Two options, analyst Mode 3 preferred: (a) audit Chernogor & Holub (2024) for the actual reported Kp or selection criteria; if Kp was genuinely ≤ 3 over the analysis window, add a sentence in detail_evidence acknowledging the dome author's dispute and citing the paper's quiet-period justification. (b) If Kp was actually elevated (5-6) and the paper reported ±10 nT anyway, remove the 'Kp ≤ 3' claim from detail_evidence and shift argument to: 'even during moderately disturbed conditions, the observed pattern falls inside Chapman's predicted 5-30 nT range.' Option (b) is safer and matches what FAIL-007 says. Either resolution closes the internal tension.

holes_found.description: UNGROUNDED SELF-RETRACTION LANGUAGE. detail_verdict_text contains: 'the FSF = H(r)/r station-to-station ratio is a genuine geometric derivation from dome parameters (Pearson r ≈ 0.9999, verified independently in the WIN-010/WIN-068 cluster) — we retract our earlier characterization of FSF as empirical.' WIN-025's current detail_evidence does NOT characterize FSF as empirical; it does not introduce FSF at all. The 'we retract our earlier characterization' hangs on a referent that no longer exists in the live text. A reader arriving at WIN-025 without having read WIN-010/WIN-068 sees a retraction of something never asserted and is left puzzled or, worse, suspicious that the review is quietly rewriting its history. This is a cluster-vocabulary-import artifact: EXP-229 copied the retraction language from the WIN-010/WIN-068 reviews where it had a live referent.
holes_found.recommendation: Two options: (a) delete the '— we retract our earlier characterization of FSF as empirical' clause in WIN-025 since WIN-025's evidence never made the empirical claim; concede FSF directly without retraction language: '...is a genuine geometric derivation from dome parameters (Pearson r ≈ 0.9999, verified in the WIN-010/WIN-068 cluster).' (b) If cluster-consistent language is preferred, add one sentence to detail_evidence introducing FSF and labeling it empirical, so the retraction has a referent. Option (a) is cleaner and reduces detail_verdict_text length by ~70 chars.

holes_found.description: 'INTRODUCED SPECIFICALLY TO REPAIR' OVERSTATES THE EVOLUTION-LOG RECORD. detail_verdict_text asserts: 'FSF = H(r)/r was introduced specifically to repair that failure.' raw-text/06-evolution.txt line 21 records the v31 failure ('Lacked specific altitude vector math. Incorrectly predicted 2024 eclipse magnitudes identically across all latitudes') and v45 introduced 'Formulas: f = c/2D, delta_Z = eclipse - quiet.' But raw-text/03-predictions.txt labels FSF = H(r)/r as 'V12 H(r)/r dome geometry' — H(r)/r itself predates v31. What v45 plausibly introduced is the eclipse-specific delta_Z = baseline × coverage × FSF formula that APPLIES H(r)/r to eclipse scaling. A precise dome defender will respond: 'H(r)/r has always been part of the geometry; the thing you are calling FSF is the same function that was there at V12. You cannot claim our fix was introduced to repair the failure when the geometric function predates the failure.' The argument still lands if rephrased to 'the eclipse-specific application of FSF was introduced to repair that failure,' but the current wording is attackable.
holes_found.recommendation: Minor-edit detail_verdict_text. Replace 'FSF = H(r)/r was introduced specifically to repair that failure' with 'the eclipse-specific delta_Z = baseline × coverage × FSF formula applying H(r)/r to eclipse scaling was introduced at v45 specifically to repair that failure, against the same 9-station data now counted as confirmation.' This is historically accurate and still carries the curve-fit-to-existing-data force of the original sentence.

holes_found.description: ARCHITECTURAL DISJOINT BETWEEN detail_evidence AND detail_verdict_text PERSISTS (c3 H3 RESIDUAL). detail_evidence was expanded by the dome-removal note (~317 chars added) but not by the FSF architecture introduction c3 H3 requested. detail_evidence still describes WIN-025 purely as 'The dome model observes that eclipses produce ~10 nT variations — known since the 1930s — and relabels the cause as aetheric.' detail_verdict_text then introduces FSF, scaling equation, retraction, Pearson r = 0.9999, BOU anchor, n(r) contamination. A reader who expands Evidence-then-Verdict still sees two arguments about different objects: evidence argues 'Chapman relabel,' verdict argues 'FSF-scaling-yes-but-magnitude-no + coordinate contamination.' EXP-229 added substantial concession/sophistication to detail_verdict_text without matching framing in detail_evidence.
holes_found.recommendation: Add 2-3 sentences to detail_evidence introducing the scaling equation and the BOU anchor so the verdict's concession lands on something the evidence already set up. Draft insertion point: after the 'known since the 1930s and relabels the cause as aetheric' closing paragraph, add: 'The dome also publishes a station-scaling equation for eclipse amplitudes: delta_Z = baseline × coverage × FSF with FSF = H(r)/r derived from V12 dome geometry (see 03-predictions.txt). The baseline is imported from the 2017 Boulder observation (−10.9 nT); the FSF station-to-station ratio is a genuine dome-geometric derivation. We address both in the Verdict below.' This closes the c3 H3 disjoint without requiring tldr_evidence changes.

holes_found.description: TLDR_VERDICT UNCHANGED FROM c3 (LENGTH 360, c3 H5 RESIDUAL). The tldr_verdict still reads: 'This is a 93-year-old standard physics prediction (Chapman 1933; Kim & Chang 2018) that the dome relabels. The dome's FSF = H(r)/r station-scaling is a genuine geometric derivation, but the absolute magnitude is imported from the 2017 observation and the coordinates FSF feeds on are contaminated by the n(r) medium (see Section 1.8 — The Coordinate Collapse).' A skim-reader arriving at WIN-025 without having read WIN-010 sees '2017 observation' and may reasonably wonder what that has to do with a 2024 9-station WIN. The cluster framing is accurate but non-self-contained in the TLDR slot that is explicitly meant for non-science-reader parsing.
holes_found.recommendation: Low priority. If another pass happens, draft: '93-year-old standard Sq physics (Chapman 1933) predicts the ±10 nT range; Chernogor & Holub themselves attribute the 2024 9-station pattern to ionospheric conductivity, not aether. The dome's station-scaling is genuine geometry but its absolute magnitude rides on the 2017 Boulder observation, not a dome derivation.' This leads with the 2024-scoped point and drops the n(r) forward reference that requires the reader to already know Section 1.8.

holes_found.description: REDUNDANT PHRASING: 'regardless of whether the claim is currently claimed.' detail_evidence closes its removal note with: 'our verdict stands regardless of whether the claim is currently claimed.' The verb 'claim' appearing twice in six words is awkward and reads like a typo survivor. A dome defender will not attack it substantively but a reader may pause on it and lose the rhetorical thread.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace with: 'our verdict stands regardless of whether the dome currently advances this as a confirmed prediction.' or shorter: 'our verdict stands whether or not the dome still lists it.'

holes_found.description: CITATION CHAIN-OF-IMPORT RISK (c3 H6 CONTINUING). Kim & Chang (2018) Advances in Space Research 61:2040-2054 is now imported into WIN-025 via EXP-229; WIN-068 c9 citation_check listed this publisher-page metadata as unverified. WIN-010, WIN-025, WIN-068 now share an identical unverified-metadata citation. If the volume/page turns out to be wrong, the fix will be cluster-wide not WIN-specific. Not an error on WIN-025 specifically, but EXP-229 extended our exposure without verification.
holes_found.recommendation: Low-priority: a single publisher-page lookup (ScienceDirect or Wiley) can resolve this once for the whole cluster. Recommend a separate EXP that verifies Chapman 1933 venue, Kim & Chang 2018 volume/page, Takeda & Araki 1984 venue in one pass.

stronger_arguments.description: EXPLICIT CHERNOGOR & HOLUB ATTRIBUTION QUOTE. detail_evidence says Chernogor & Holub 'attribute the observed effects to ionospheric conductivity changes — not to any aetheric mechanism.' If the paper contains a direct attribution quote (e.g., 'consistent with Sq current disruption during totality'), quoting it verbatim is more damaging than a paraphrase, because it shows the dome is counting as 'aetheric confirmation' an observation the authors themselves explicitly labeled ionospheric.
sources: Chernogor & Holub (2024) doi:10.1016/j.asr.2024.10.056 — fetch abstract / discussion section for attribution language

stronger_arguments.description: PRE-V45 PREDICTION ARCHIVE / OTS ANCHOR CHECK. The dome's '93-year-old Sq prediction' argument is strengthened if we can show that the dome's pre-v45 latitude-uniform 2024 prediction was git-committed / timestamped BEFORE the 2024 eclipse, then silently replaced by FSF-scaled values after the Chernogor & Holub paper appeared. If git log on monitor.py or the predictions page shows the v31-era forecast values were ±10 nT uniform across stations (not latitude-scaled), and the post-2024-paper version shows latitude-scaled values matching the observed pattern, that is a curve-fit-after-the-fact signature stronger than the evolution-log admission alone.
sources: john09289/predictions git history for monitor.py and data/predictions.txt pre-2024-10 vs post-2024-10
raw-text/06-evolution.txt v31 row vs v45 row

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome's FSF = H(r)/r station-scaling IS a genuine dome-geometric derivation, and EXP-229 correctly concedes this. The Pearson r ≈ 0.9999 fit between FSF and observed station amplitudes (WIN-010/WIN-068 cluster verification) is real. The dome also correctly notes that the magnetic signal 'tracks eclipse geometry, not solar noon,' which is a non-trivial observable prediction that the model does capture — albeit the same observable is predicted by Chapman Sq physics by construction.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: A genuine geometric scaling is necessary but not sufficient for a confirmed prediction. The dome's station-to-station ratio matches the observed ratio, but standard ionospheric Sq current physics predicts the same ratio (the shadow traverses the ionosphere, conductivity drop scales with totality coverage). Both models predict the same observable pattern; the dome neither produces an absolute-magnitude prediction (imported from 2017) nor proposes a test that distinguishes aetheric from Sq mechanisms. Confirmed prediction requires a prediction that could fail and did not; here the dome is co-predicting with standard physics on an observation that standard physics predicted 93 years earlier.

deeper_analysis: Net assessment of the EXP-229 patch batch: substantive improvement, residual polish needed. The three strongest attacks a dome defender could run on pre-EXP-229 text — (i) 'you refute a claim I already dropped,' (ii) 'FSF is genuine geometry so your WIN-010/WIN-025/WIN-068 critique double-counts the physics,' (iii) 'your pre-EXP-229 FSF-empirical characterization was wrong' — are now mostly defused. The dome-removal annotation in detail_evidence kills (i). The FSF concession in detail_verdict_text kills (ii) architecturally and redirects the critique to the absolute-magnitude anchor and the n(r) coordinate-contamination argument. (iii) is more awkward post-patch because the retraction language ('we retract our earlier characterization of FSF as empirical') imports a referent that does not exist in WIN-025's own live text — a cluster-vocabulary-import artifact. The evolution-log admission (strongest affirmative attack) made it into detail_verdict_text but not into the finding row, so the summary-table view loses the strongest point. Separately the internal Kp inconsistency (detail_evidence says Kp ≤ 3, detail_extra/FAIL-007 say Kp ~5-6) is the most dangerous residual hole — a dome defender seeing both within one review will lead with it. The triple-count closing sentence was rewritten to survive the FSF concession and that rewrite holds up: 'one physical process, three aggregation scales, three counts of confirmation' is the right framing. CONSIDERING THE OTHER SIDE: a charitable defender can legitimately argue that (a) the FSF geometric derivation is a real contribution that the original critique underweighted and EXP-229 partially concedes this, (b) 'triple-counting' can be parsed as 'three independent datasets corroborating the same mechanism,' which is a normal and accepted scientific procedure, and (c) the dome author's voluntary removal of WIN-025 on data-quality grounds is real self-correction that deserves more credit than a single sentence of detail_extra. Our review acknowledges (c) but doesn't lean on it in the finding or tldr. Overall the WIN is closer to bulletproof than before but not there yet; one more targeted edit pass on finding (import evolution-log admission), detail_evidence (introduce FSF architecture + resolve Kp), and detail_verdict_text (drop ungrounded retraction + soften 'introduced' wording) would close most of the residual holes. Verdict Std Model Explains is unambiguously correct and should not move.

Kim & Chang (2018) Advances in Space Research 61:2040-2054 — volume/page metadata unverified at cluster level (c3 H6 residual)
